title: Bioschemas/schema.org export + protocols.io import/export round-trip
context: Interoperability with existing ecosystems (Bioschemas/schema.org and protocols.io) is the M3 strategic goal that makes the corpus reusable beyond the project itself and supports a clean handoff to an adopting repository. The crosswalk to Bioschemas LabProtocol was designed in schema-001; this task implements the export and validates a protocols.io import-to-export round-trip without losing safety or provenance fields. Depends on schema-001 (data model and crosswalk) and render-010 (renderer infrastructure).
objective: Implement Bioschemas LabProtocol / schema.org JSON-LD export from protocol records, and implement a protocols.io import adaptor with a tested round-trip (protocols.io import → our schema → export) that preserves all safety and provenance fields.
acceptanceCriteria: protocol render --format jsonld produces a valid Bioschemas LabProtocol / schema.org JSON-LD document from a protocol record.
A protocols.io import adaptor converts a protocols.io JSON export to the lab-protocols-open schema without losing safety/provenance fields; round-trip test committed.
Safety and provenance fields are preserved through the import-to-export round-trip; any unmappable fields are logged as warnings, not silently dropped.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
A brief interop guide documents the export/import commands and any known field mapping limitations.
resources: lab-protocols-open schema-001 (Protocol JSON Schema and Bioschemas crosswalk)
output: A merged PR adding JSON-LD export and a protocols.io import adaptor with a round-trip test, interop guide, and CI integration; commit DCO signed-off.
